name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America Metairie, Louisiana — Advance America is one of the largest U.S. payday and installment lenders, offering small-dollar loans in-store an...
description_long:
Founded in 1997 and headquartered in Greenville, South Carolina, Advance America operates as a subsidiary of Purpose Financial under CEO J. Patrick O'Shaughnessy. With over 800 storefronts spanning 27 states, it ranks among the largest state-licensed consumer lenders in the country. The company holds an A+ BBB rating and earned formal BBB accreditation in September 2024. It operates exclusively under state-level consumer lending regulations — it holds no CDFI certification, HUD approval, or NFCC membership.

Advance America's core offering is short-term, small-dollar lending for individuals who need quick cash. Payday loans typically carry a fee of around $15 per $100 borrowed, with APRs ranging from 143.29% to 688.28% depending on the state. State laws shape the product significantly: California caps loans at $255, Florida at $500 (with a 10% fee plus $5 verification), and Ohio limits installment loan APRs to 28%. Beyond payday loans, the company offers installment loans with fixed monthly payments over longer terms, revolving lines of credit, and title loans brokered through LoanCenter. In-store locations also provide check cashing and general money services.

What sets Advance America apart in the short-term lending market is its scale and dual-channel accessibility. Customers can apply in person at one of 800+ branches or online through the company's portal at online.advanceamerica.net, which supports full loan management, repayment tracking, and MFA-secured account access. Same-day funding is available for approvals submitted before 10:30 AM ET, with funds deposited by 5 PM ET the same day. Many states allow payday loan applications without a hard credit pull, making it accessible to borrowers with poor or thin credit histories who would be declined by traditional banks.

Advance America's primary strength is accessibility — fast approvals, widespread physical presence, and no hard credit pull in many states make it a practical option when no other short-term credit is available. However, the costs are steep and the risks real. APRs regularly exceed 400% and in some products approach 688%, meaning even short repayment windows can produce significant total repayment amounts — a $650 installment loan in South Carolina, for example, totals $1,341.12 over six months. BBB customer reviews tell a different story than the A+ accreditation: 154 reviewers gave an average of 1.7 out of 5 stars, and 302 complaints were filed over three years. Full loan terms are disclosed only after the application process begins, limiting upfront comparison shopping. This is an emergency financial tool, not a cost-effective borrowing solution.

pricing.guarantee_details: No general money-back guarantee. Some states mandate partial fee refunds if a payday loan is repaid before the due date — this is state law, not a company policy.

tiers.name: Payday Loans
features: No hard credit pull in many states
Same-day funding available for AM approvals

tiers.name: Installment Loans
features: Fixed monthly payments over multi-month terms
Example: $650 loan in SC = $1,341.12 total over 6 months (348% APR)
Ohio capped at 28% APR by state law
Longer repayment window than payday loans

tiers.name: Lines of Credit
features: Interest charged only on amount drawn

tiers.name: Title Loans
features: Secured loan using vehicle equity as collateral
Larger loan amounts than unsecured payday products
Vehicle must be owned outright or have sufficient equity

pros: 800+ physical storefronts in 27 states — one of the largest payday lender footprints in the U.S.
Same-day funding for online approvals submitted before 10:30 AM ET
No hard credit pull required for payday loans in many states
BBB A+ rating with formal accreditation since September 2024
Nearly 30 years in operation since 1997 — established regulatory compliance history
Dual-channel access: apply in-store or fully online via secure portal
Complaint volume trending downward — 302 over 3 years, 74 in last 12 months
cons: APRs range from 143.29% to 688.28% — among the highest cost borrowing in consumer finance
BBB customer review rating is only 1.7/5 based on 154 reviews, despite the A+ accreditation
Full loan terms and rates are disclosed only after the application is submitted — limited upfront transparency
Title loans are not offered directly — routed through third-party partner LoanCenter
No dedicated mobile app confirmed — limited to web-based portal for digital management
best_for: Consumers facing a short-term cash shortfall before their next paycheck with no other credit options
Borrowers with poor or thin credit histories who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans
People who prefer or need in-person financial services near one of 800+ store locations
Individuals in states where Advance America operates and who need same-day emergency funds
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for consumers in a genuine short-term financial emergency who have exhausted lower-cost options — particularly those with poor credit who need cash today and have access to a local branch or can apply online. The main caveat is steep: APRs routinely exceed 400%, and the total repayment cost on even a modest installment loan can be double the principal, making this an expensive last resort rather than a routine borrowing tool.
services: Payday loans (short-term, repaid on next payday)
Installment loans (fixed monthly payments, multi-month terms)
Lines of credit (revolving, interest on drawn balance only)
Same-day loan funding (approvals before 10:30 AM ET)
Online loan application and account management
In-store loan applications with local staff
Loan repayment management via online portal

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
